payload.source_text: High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.
High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.
High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.description: Task claiming process performed by children

payload.description: Task and reward establishment process performed by parents
payload.justification: Signal describes parental process of establishing tasks with monetary reward values.

payload.description: Pocket money disbursement process performed by parents
payload.justification: Signal identifies disbursement as strategic process facilitated by reporting visibility.

payload.description: Visibility provision function for task status and availability
payload.justification: Signal describes strategic function of providing children visibility into task information.

payload.description: Earnings totals display function for financial awareness
payload.justification: Signal articulates function of showing weekly earnings to support financial awareness goal.

payload.description: Historical record maintenance function for accountability
payload.justification: Signal identifies maintenance of historical records as strategic function supporting tracking and accountability.

payload.description: Parent as strategic participant in pocket money system

payload.description: Pocket money task as work unit of strategic importance
payload.justification: Tasks are the core entity children complete, parents establish with monetary values, and children view for awareness.

payload.description: Weekly earnings total as strategic financial metric
payload.justification: Signal identifies weekly earnings totals as distinct entity shown to children for financial awareness.

payload.description: Historical record as strategic accountability artifact
payload.justification: Signal establishes historical records as entities maintained for tracking and accountability purposes.

payload.description: Task completion status as strategic state indicator
payload.justification: Signals reference completed versus available tasks, indicating completion status as key strategic attribute.

payload.description: Weekly cycle for task claiming and earnings accumulation
payload.justification: Signals reference task claiming throughout the week and weekly earnings totals, establishing weekly cycle as strategic timing.

payload.description: Child role as task claimant and earnings viewer
payload.justification: Signals establish child as strategic role performing task claiming, viewing tasks, and seeing earnings.

payload.description: Parent role as reward definer and disbursement facilitator

payload.description: Facilitate accurate pocket money disbursement through reporting

payload.description: Maintain historical accountability through record-keeping

payload.description: Historical data retention requirement

payload.description: Weekly reset boundary
payload.justification: Business trigger at period boundaries initiating the reset of all task entities to available state.

payload.description: Task completion
payload.justification: Business event occurring when a child completes a claimed task, associating it with that child entity.

payload.description: Child user
payload.justification: Business actor who claims tasks, views task status, and receives weekly earnings within the pocket money system.

payload.description: Parent user

payload.description: Enable children to claim and complete pocket money tasks
payload.justification: Core business goal defining the system's purpose of providing a pocket money task management capability for children.

payload.description: Facilitate parent oversight and payment fulfillment
payload.justification: Business goal enabling parents to monitor task completion and fulfill payment obligations based on consolidated reports.

payload.description: Tasks must reset weekly
payload.justification: Business constraint requiring all task entities to return to available state at weekly period boundaries.

payload.description: Historical task data must be retained
payload.justification: Business constraint mandating that historical task completion records remain accessible beyond current operational periods.

payload.description: Task claiming process enabling children to mark tasks as completed
payload.justification: Signals describe the core process flow for children claiming completed tasks

payload.description: Task creation process enabling parents to define tasks with monetary values
payload.justification: Signals define the logical process by which parents create task definitions

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregation process calculating period-end totals
payload.justification: Signals specify the temporal aggregation logic for calculating weekly earnings | SG959 specifies temporal aggregation logic for calculating weekly earnings, representing a logical process that computes earnings over a week.

payload.description: Task visibility function providing children access to available and completed task collections
payload.justification: Signals define visibility requirements as a functional capability for children

payload.description: Weekly task reset rule transitioning all tasks to available state
payload.justification: Signals specify the reset logic as a normative rule governing task state transitions

payload.description: Historical data retention rule mandating accessibility beyond current period
payload.justification: Signals mandate retention as a normative rule governing data lifecycle | SG962 mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data, representing a logical business rule governing data preservation.

payload.description: Parent reporting process generating child-specific earnings and completion summaries
payload.justification: Signals define the reporting process providing parents with analytics data | SG960 defines the reporting process that provides earnings and task data to parents, representing a logical process for report generation.

payload.description: Task state reset process
payload.justification: SG961 specifies weekly reset logic that transitions tasks to available state, representing a logical process for resetting task states.

payload.description: Task logical entity representing assignable household chores
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference tasks as the core data entity with states, monetary values, and lifecycle transitions

payload.description: Monetary value attribute associated with task entities
payload.justification: Signals explicitly describe tasks having associated monetary values as a defining attribute

payload.description: Task state attribute tracking available versus completed status
payload.justification: Signals describe tasks transitioning between available and completed states, indicating a state attribute

payload.description: Child earnings logical entity representing accumulated monetary totals
payload.justification: Signals reference calculation and reporting of earnings as a distinct aggregated data entity

payload.description: Historical task data entity for retention and archival beyond current period
payload.justification: Signals mandate retention of historical task data, implying a distinct archived entity | SG962 mandates retention of historical task data beyond current period, implying a logical entity that stores historical task information.

payload.description: Completion relationship linking task entities to child actors
payload.justification: Signals explicitly specify the relationship mechanism connecting completed tasks to children

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregate
payload.justification: SG959 describes temporal aggregation logic for weekly earnings, indicating a logical data entity that represents calculated earnings over a weekly period.

payload.description: Child-specific earnings amount
payload.justification: SG960 specifies child-specific earnings data provided in reporting, representing an attribute of earnings associated with individual children.

payload.description: Task completion status
payload.justification: SG960 defines task completion data as part of reporting output, representing a logical attribute capturing the completion state of tasks.

payload.description: Task availability state
payload.justification: SG961 specifies transition of tasks to available state, indicating a logical attribute representing task availability status.

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle defining the temporal period for task availability and earnings calculation
payload.justification: Signals repeatedly reference the weekly cycle as the temporal boundary for operations | SG961 specifies weekly reset logic, representing a logical cycle that repeats on a weekly basis for task state management.

payload.description: Period end event triggering earnings aggregation calculation
payload.justification: Signals specify temporal aggregation at period end, implying a defined event | SG959 specifies calculation at period end, representing a logical event that marks the conclusion of a weekly earnings period.

payload.description: Weekly reset trigger initiating task state transition to available
payload.justification: Signals describe the weekly reset as a triggering condition for state transitions | SG961 specifies reset logic that transitions task states, representing a logical trigger that initiates the weekly state transition.

payload.description: Child actor responsible for claiming and completing tasks
payload.justification: Signals consistently reference children as the primary actor claiming tasks and viewing data

payload.description: Parent actor responsible for creating tasks and reviewing reports
payload.justification: Signals reference parents as the actor creating task definitions and consuming reporting outputs

payload.description: Parent user role
payload.justification: SG960 defines reporting provided to parents, indicating a logical role that receives child-specific earnings and task data.

payload.description: Data visibility constraint ensuring children access both available and completed task sets
payload.justification: Signals define visibility requirements as a design constraint on data access

payload.description: Historical retention constraint requiring accessibility of task data beyond current period
payload.justification: Signals mandate retention as a normative constraint on data lifecycle management | SG962 mandates accessibility of historical task data, representing a design constraint requiring data to remain accessible beyond the current period.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Participant Stewardship
payload.description: Enterprise accountability for defining and maintaining the roles, relationships, and responsibilities of family members who participate in the household economic system, establishing clear boundaries of authority and entitlement.

payload.name: Work-Reward Obligation Framework
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to establishing and governing the relationship between defined work units and their corresponding monetary entitlements, including the rules for defining, claiming, and fulfilling these obligations.

payload.name: Financial Disbursement Accountability
payload.description: Enterprise responsibility for accurate calculation, transparent reporting, and timely transfer of earned monetary entitlements according to established performance records and periodic settlement cycles.

payload.name: Transparency and Information Stewardship
payload.description: Enterprise obligation to provide participants with visibility into available opportunities, current status of commitments, and financial positions to enable informed participation and decision-making within the system.

payload.name: Historical Record Accountability
payload.description: Enterprise duty to maintain persistent, auditable records of all completed obligations and financial transactions, ensuring long-term accountability, dispute resolution capability, and institutional memory.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining, governing, and maintaining the lifecycle of work obligations that children may undertake for compensation, including establishment of value, completion criteria, and recurring availability patterns.

payload.name: Child Participation Entitlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for enabling children to engage with available work obligations, claim those they will perform, execute them, and establish their entitlement to compensation through demonstrated completion.

payload.name: Earnings Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for determining, accumulating, and reporting the monetary obligations owed to participants based on their completed work within defined temporal boundaries, enabling settlement fulfillment.

payload.name: Parental Oversight
payload.description: Business responsibility for providing visibility into child participation patterns, completion achievements, and fulfillment obligations to enable informed parental governance and payment execution.

payload.name: Historical Record Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for preserving evidence of past task definitions, participation events, and completion outcomes across temporal boundaries to support accountability, pattern analysis, and governance continuity.

payload.name: Task Lifecycle Model
payload.description: Logical structure and state transitions governing task entities from creation through completion, including availability states, completion status tracking, claiming behavior, and weekly reset cycles that return tasks to available state.

payload.name: Earnings Derivation Model
payload.description: Logical relationships and aggregation logic linking completed task monetary values to child-specific earnings accumulation, including weekly period-end totals and the derivation rules that transform task completion events into calculated earnings amounts.

payload.name: Task Visibility Model
payload.description: Logical access model defining how child actors view and interact with task collections, enforcing constraints that ensure visibility of both available and completed task sets and controlling which task information is accessible based on logical actor role boundaries.

payload.name: Parent Visibility Model
payload.description: Logical reporting structure enabling parent actors to access child-specific earnings summaries and task completion status, organized by the logical role boundaries that distinguish parent oversight capabilities from child operational interactions.

payload.name: Historical Data Retention Model
payload.description: Logical structure and constraints governing task data persistence beyond the current operational period, including the archival entity structure and the integrity rules mandating accessibility of completed task history for longitudinal visibility.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise children as participants in the household economic system.
payload.rationale: Establishes the Child entity as a structural participant within the enterprise scope, providing the foundation for role-based obligations.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise parents as participants in the household economic system.
payload.rationale: Establishes the Parent entity as a structural participant within the enterprise scope, providing the foundation for role-based obligations.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim tasks.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to provide the Child role with task claiming capability, derived from the role definition as task claimant.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to view earnings.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to provide the Child role with earnings viewing capability, derived from the role definition as earnings viewer.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to define rewards.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to provide the Parent role with reward definition capability, derived from the role definition as reward definer.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall facilitate disbursement of rewards.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to facilitate disbursement, derived from the role definition of Parent as disbursement facilitator.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish tasks as work units.
payload.rationale: Entity definition at enterprise scope — task is the work unit the enterprise recognises.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish monetary reward amounts as attributes of tasks.
payload.rationale: Structural relationship between task entity and its monetary attribute at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain task completion status.
payload.rationale: Task completion status is a strategic state attribute the enterprise must track.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim tasks.
payload.rationale: How-column process commitment — enterprise enables the claiming capability without prescribing system implementation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to establish tasks.
payload.rationale: How-column process commitment — enterprise enables parent capability to define work units.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to establish rewards.
payload.rationale: How-column process commitment — enterprise enables parent capability to define reward units.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a task-based monetary reward system enabling children to claim completed pocket money tasks.
payload.rationale: Why-column goal expressing the governing commitment — establishing the system is the enterprise-level policy constraint.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a weekly settlement cycle for task claiming.
payload.rationale: Defines the temporal governance constraint for the task claiming cycle at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a weekly settlement cycle for earnings accumulation.
payload.rationale: Defines the temporal governance constraint for the earnings accumulation cycle at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain weekly earnings totals as financial accountability metrics.
payload.rationale: Establishes the structural obligation for the core financial metric entity required for disbursement accountability.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to view their earnings totals.
payload.rationale: Functional capability derived from How-column display function, supporting the Why-column goal of building financial awareness through earnings visibility.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide for pocket money disbursement by parents based on reported earnings.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to enable the disbursement process, supporting the Why-column goal of facilitating accurate pocket money disbursement through reporting.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children with visibility into available tasks.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to enable informed participation by ensuring children can see task opportunities. Derived from the visibility provision function and the goal of task awareness.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children with visibility into claimed tasks.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to enable children to track their claimed commitments. Derived from the visibility provision function and the goal of task awareness.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children with visibility into their earnings.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to support financial literacy by enabling children to view their accumulated earnings and understand the financial outcomes of completed tasks.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain persistent records of all completed obligations.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise's commitment to maintain historical records of completed obligations. All three CCIs support this obligation: the What-column identifies the historical record artifact, the How-column addresses the maintenance function, and the Why-column establishes the accountability purpose.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain persistent records of all financial transactions.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise's commitment to maintain historical records of financial transactions. All three CCIs support this obligation: the What-column identifies the historical record artifact, the How-column addresses the maintenance function, and the Why-column establishes the accountability purpose.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall ensure historical records are auditable.
payload.rationale: Constrains the quality of maintained records to be auditable, which is a governance rule on how records must be kept. Both CCIs contribute to this auditability constraint: the What-column characterizes the record as an accountability artifact and the Why-column establishes accountability as the governing principle.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain historical records to support dispute resolution.
payload.rationale: Constrains the purpose for which historical records must be maintained—dispute resolution support. This is a governance rule expressing the Why obligation: accountability for resolving disputes requires records to be kept.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain historical records to support institutional memory.
payload.rationale: Constrains the purpose for which historical records must be maintained—institutional memory support. This is a governance rule expressing the Why obligation: accountability for preserving institutional knowledge requires records to be kept.

payload.statement: The system shall recognise Child as a participant entity.
payload.rationale: Elaborates enterprise recognition of children into a structural business obligation to maintain Child as an entity.

payload.statement: The system shall recognise Parent as a participant entity.
payload.rationale: Elaborates enterprise recognition of parents into a structural business obligation to maintain Parent as an entity.

payload.statement: The system shall make tasks claimable by child users.
payload.rationale: Expresses the system-level affordance that enables the child claiming capability.

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks visible to child users.
payload.rationale: Expresses the system-level affordance that enables children to view available tasks.

payload.statement: The system shall make completed tasks visible to child users.
payload.rationale: Expresses the system-level affordance that enables children to view their completed tasks.

payload.statement: The system shall make task definition available to parent users.
payload.rationale: Expresses the system-level affordance that enables parents to define tasks with rewards.

payload.statement: The business shall be accountable for determining weekly earnings amounts.
payload.rationale: Elaborates enterprise disbursement facilitation into business responsibility for calculating earnings owed.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain Task as an entity.
payload.rationale: Establishes Task as a structural entity the business maintains.

payload.statement: Task shall have a monetary value attribute.
payload.rationale: Establishes monetary value as a structural attribute of Task.

payload.statement: Task shall have a completion status attribute.
payload.rationale: Establishes completion status as a structural attribute of Task.

payload.statement: Parent shall be related to Task through a defines relationship.
payload.rationale: Establishes the structural relationship enabling parents to establish rewards on tasks.

payload.statement: The business shall enable children to claim completed pocket money tasks for monetary reward.
payload.rationale: Expresses the governing business goal that constrains the task-based reward system design.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly reset cycle for task claiming.
payload.rationale: Expresses the temporal constraint governing task claiming within weekly boundaries.
payload.fit_criteria: Tasks shall reset at each weekly reset boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall be accountable for determining weekly earnings amounts within each weekly period.
payload.rationale: Elaborates weekly settlement cycle for earnings into business responsibility for calculating earnings within the cycle.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of weekly earnings totals for each child.
payload.rationale: Row 2 elaboration of the enterprise commitment to maintain weekly earnings totals; expresses the business responsibility to record these metrics for accountability.

payload.statement: The system shall make earnings totals visible to the entitled child.
payload.rationale: Row 2 system-subject affordance complementing the child's viewing action; black-box capability provided at the boundary.

payload.statement: The system shall make reported earnings available to parents for disbursement decisions.
payload.rationale: Row 2 system-subject affordance complementing parental disbursement; provides the visibility capability at the boundary.

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks visible to child users.
payload.rationale: Row 2 system-subject affordance complementing child's viewing capability; black-box presentation of available tasks.

payload.statement: The system shall make claimed tasks visible to the child who claimed them.
payload.rationale: Row 2 system-subject affordance complementing child's viewing capability; black-box presentation of claimed tasks.

payload.statement: The system shall make earnings visible to the entitled child.
payload.rationale: Row 2 system-subject affordance complementing child's earnings viewing capability; black-box presentation.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each completed task.
payload.rationale: Row 2 business responsibility to record completed work obligations; supports accountability and historical evidence.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each earnings calculation.
payload.rationale: Row 2 business responsibility to record financial compensation determinations; supports financial accountability.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain historical task data.
payload.rationale: Row 2 business responsibility to preserve past task evidence; foundation for auditability constraint.

payload.statement: The business shall ensure historical task data is auditable.
payload.rationale: Row 2 constraint on historical record quality; requires preserved data to support examination and verification.
payload.fit_criteria: Historical task records can be examined and their integrity verified.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain historical task data to support dispute resolution.
payload.rationale: Row 2 constraint expressing the purpose-driven retention requirement; historical data must serve dispute adjudication needs.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain historical task data to support institutional memory.
payload.rationale: Row 2 constraint expressing the purpose-driven retention requirement; historical data must serve organizational continuity needs.

payload.statement: The system shall make tasks definable by parent users.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance providing the black-box capability that enables the parent actor to define tasks. Complementary to the actor requirement — the system provides WHAT (task definition capability), not HOW it is implemented.

payload.statement: A Task shall comprise a monetary value attribute.
payload.rationale: Structural assertion derived from object-recursion on the Task entity: What-column entity (Task) and its attribute (monetary value) express a composition obligation.

payload.statement: A Task shall comprise a completion status attribute.
payload.rationale: Structural assertion for the Task entity's lifecycle state attribute, enabling tracking of work obligation completion.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly task reset cycle.
payload.rationale: Business-subject governance constraint expressing the recurring availability pattern obligation. Why-column CCI (tasks must reset weekly) establishes the constraint nature; How/Rule CCI, When/Cycle, When/Event, and When/Trigger CCIs together define the reset responsibility as a business rule enforcement obligation.
payload.fit_criteria: Task availability status resets at the weekly reset boundary for all recurring tasks.

payload.statement: The system shall make pocket money tasks claimable by children.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement: black-box affordance enabling the claim capability at the boundary (How column capability declaration supported by Why column goal).

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks visible to children.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement: black-box affordance providing visibility of available tasks at the boundary (How column capability declaration).

payload.statement: The system shall make completed tasks visible to children.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement: black-box affordance providing visibility of completed tasks at the boundary (How column capability declaration).

payload.statement: The business shall recognise child as a participant entity.
payload.rationale: Structural assertion: the business must recognise and maintain the concept of a child entity within its model (What column entity).

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each child's task completion.
payload.rationale: Business-subject responsibility: maintaining the completion relationship behind the boundary (What column relationship + When column event trigger).

payload.statement: The business shall calculate weekly earnings for each participant based on completed work.
payload.rationale: How-column process obligation defining the business capability to determine monetary obligations owed to participants.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a weekly earnings amount for each participant.
payload.rationale: What-column attribute defining the business artefact that records the calculated monetary obligation.

payload.statement: The business shall govern earnings calculation within weekly period boundaries.
payload.rationale: When-column temporal constraint defining the cycle and event boundaries within which earnings accountability operates.

payload.statement: The business shall make earnings information visible to parents.
payload.rationale: Why-column goal expressing the business capability to provide parental visibility of earnings information.

payload.statement: The business shall facilitate parental oversight of child earnings.
payload.rationale: Why-column governance goal establishing the business responsibility to enable oversight, supporting the accountability chain from calculation to settlement.

payload.statement: The business shall facilitate payment fulfillment for child earnings.
payload.rationale: Why-column governance goal establishing the business responsibility to enable payment settlement.

payload.statement: The system shall make consolidated completion reports visible to the parent.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance statement expressing the black-box capability the system provides at the boundary to enable parental oversight. Complementary to the actor-subject requirement — the system-side of the same interaction.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a parent entity with governance responsibilities for child participation.
payload.rationale: Structural assertion derived from the What-column entity (Parent). The entity represents the business role with governance responsibilities, distinct from the actor capability.

payload.statement: The business shall provide visibility into child participation patterns.
payload.rationale: Business-subject capability derived from the Why-column goal. Expresses the business responsibility to enable parental oversight of child participation.

payload.statement: The business shall provide visibility into child completion achievements.
payload.rationale: Business-subject capability derived from the Why-column goal. Expresses the business responsibility to enable parental oversight of child achievements to inform payment fulfillment.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of historical task definitions.
payload.rationale: What-column entity representing the business artefact (historical task definitions) that must be maintained behind the boundary. Functional because it expresses a capability to maintain a business record.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of historical participation events.
payload.rationale: What-column entity representing the business artefact (historical participation events) that must be maintained behind the boundary. Functional because it expresses a capability to maintain a business record.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of historical completion outcomes.
payload.rationale: What-column entity representing the business artefact (historical completion outcomes) that must be maintained behind the boundary. Functional because it expresses a capability to maintain a business record.

payload.statement: The business shall retain historical task data in accordance with the established retention requirement.
payload.rationale: How-column rule (CCI-007) and Why-column constraint (CCI-004) both express the same governance obligation: that historical data must be retained per a defined rule. Constraint type because this expresses a governance rule on business behaviour (data retention policy).

payload.statement: A child user can interact with the system to participate in chore and reward activities.
payload.rationale: Who-column Actor CCI describing the child user as an external party interacting with the system boundary. At Row 2, this establishes the actor's capability to engage with the system for chore participation. Subject = actor; statement uses 'can' normative form appropriate for actor-subject requirements.

payload.statement: A child user can claim an available task.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement (boundary-crossing) expressing the child user's capability to claim tasks, directly realizing the enterprise commitment to enable children to claim tasks.

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks claimable by child users.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement (black-box affordance) expressing what capability the system provides at the boundary to enable task claiming, complementary to the actor-action statement.

payload.statement: A child user can view available tasks.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing prerequisite capability for task claiming—children must be able to view available tasks before claiming them.

payload.statement: A child user can view completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement enabling children to view their completed tasks, supporting their participation in the task lifecycle after claiming.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise task completion events established by child users.
payload.rationale: Business-subject statement expressing the responsibility to recognize and record when a child completes a claimed task, necessary for establishing entitlement to compensation.

payload.statement: A task shall comprise a monetary value.
payload.rationale: Structural assertion defining a necessary attribute of the Task entity—monetary value is required for children to understand the compensation for claiming a task.

payload.statement: A task shall comprise a completion status.
payload.rationale: Structural assertion defining a necessary attribute of the Task entity—completion status is required to distinguish available from completed tasks in the claiming lifecycle.

payload.statement: A parent user can define a task.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement (boundary-crossing) expressing the parent user's capability to define tasks, directly realizing the enterprise commitment to enable parents to establish tasks.

payload.statement: The system shall make task definition capability available to parent users.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement (black-box affordance) expressing what capability the system provides at the boundary to enable task definition, complementary to the actor-action statement.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a relationship between parent and defined task.
payload.rationale: Business-subject statement expressing the responsibility to record and preserve the authorship relationship between a parent and the tasks they define, supporting governance and accountability.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly reset cycle for tasks.
payload.rationale: Constraint expressing a temporal governance rule governing task lifecycle—tasks must reset weekly at the week-end boundary, constraining how task establishment behaves across temporal periods.
payload.fit_criteria: Tasks shall reset at the weekly reset boundary.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Requirement elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: child
payload.description: The enterprise shall recognise children as participants in the household economic system.

value_set: Child actor retrieves only completed tasks where completion relationship references that child's identity.
Child actor role permissions include retrieval of task entities with available state attribute value.
Child actor role permissions include retrieval of task entities with completed state attribute value.
Child actor logical access boundary includes the available task collection.
Child actor logical access boundary includes the completed task collection.

payload.name: household economic system
payload.description: The enterprise shall recognise children as participants in the household economic system.

payload.name: parent
payload.description: The enterprise shall recognise parents as participants in the household economic system.

payload.name: task
payload.description: The enterprise shall enable children to claim tasks.

value_set: completed
available
claimed
Historical task records can be examined and their integrity verified.
Task availability status resets at the weekly reset boundary for all recurring tasks.
Tasks shall reset at the weekly reset boundary.
All completed tasks reference exactly one child actor.
Historical task data entities can be retrieved beyond their originating period.
Historical task data entities preserve completion relationships for retrieval.
Historical task data entities preserve task state attributes for retrieval.
Tasks not in available state cannot be claimed

payload.description: The business shall retain historical task data in accordance with the established retention requirement.

payload.description: The system shall maintain a logical association between Parent and Task representing the defines relationship.

payload.name: access boundary
payload.description: The system shall enforce access boundaries such that parent user roles may view child-specific data within the logical visibility model.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Establishes the core purpose of enabling children to claim completed pocket money tasks throughout the week.
Defines parental motivation to establish task-based monetary rewards with specific amounts.
Expresses the goal of providing children visibility into available and completed tasks for awareness.
Articulates the purpose of showing weekly earnings totals to children for financial awareness.
Establishes parental need for reporting visibility to facilitate accurate pocket money disbursement.
Identifies the goal of maintaining historical records for tracking or accountability purposes.
Section header indicating the beginning of a conceptual system overview.
Core business concept defining the system's purpose as enabling children to claim pocket money tasks upon completion within weekly cycles.
Business capability allowing parents to define tasks with associated monetary values as core entities.
Conceptual relationship establishing that completed tasks are associated with the child entity who performed them.
Business capability enabling children to view both available and completed task entities within the current week context.
Business capability providing children with aggregated weekly earnings calculations at the end of each week period.
Business capability enabling parents to view consolidated reports linking child entities to completed tasks and earned amounts for payment fulfillment.
Business rule defining weekly reset cycle where all task entities return to available state at period boundaries.
Business requirement mandating retention and accessibility of historical task data entities beyond current operational period.

Conceptual relationship establishing that completed tasks are associated with the child entity who performed them.

Business capability providing children with aggregated weekly earnings calculations at the end of each week period.

Business requirement mandating retention and accessibility of historical task data entities beyond current operational period.

Defines visibility requirements for children to access both available and completed task collections.
Specifies the temporal aggregation logic for calculating weekly earnings at period end.
Defines the reporting process providing parents with child-specific earnings and task completion data.
Specifies the weekly reset logic that transitions all tasks back to available state.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data beyond current period.
